candidates.why: Finance income is not operating revenue; no true revenue/sales line is present in the preview, suggesting the firm is pre-revenue or revenue is not reported separately.
Interest income is financial income, not operating revenue; not appropriate for REVT.
Net finance result, not operating revenue; unsuitable as top-line sales.
Tax line, clearly not revenue.
Represents comprehensive income, not revenue.

variables.notes: No line item corresponding to operating revenue or sales appears in the provided Income Statement preview. Available candidates are finance- or tax-related and not suitable as top-line revenue. This issuer likely has no reported revenue; thus REVT is mapped to an empty set (treated as 0).

candidates.why: Represents general payroll-related overhead, not clearly direct cost of goods/services.
Broad operating overhead category; not clearly identified as cost of goods sold.
Research and development, not cost of goods sold.
Represents total operating expenses (overheads, R&D, etc.), not strictly direct production costs.
Financial, not operating production costs.
Balance sheet accrual, not a P&L COGS line.

variables.notes: There is no explicit cost of goods sold or cost of sales line. Expenses are primarily R&D and operating overhead, consistent with a pre-revenue R&D company. Using generic operating expenses as COGS would be misleading, so COGS is set to an empty list (treated as 0).

candidates.why: Share-based compensation is a typical SG&A component (overhead).
Represents operating overhead; likely includes various SG&A-type costs not captured elsewhere.
Balance sheet item, not an income statement expense component, so unsuitable for SG&A components mapping.
Balance sheet liability, not an expense line.

variables.notes: REVIEWED. Selected multiple operating overhead components as SG&A: share-based payments, payroll-related expenses, facilities (rent, office, IT), professional fees, and other operating expenses. R&D, depreciation/amortisation, impairments, finance costs, and totals were deliberately excluded in line with instructions. | Escape-hatch used (final_choice outside candidate list): [Income Statement] Other payroll related expenses, [Income Statement] Rent, office and IT, [Income Statement] Accounting, audit, legal, consulting

candidates.why: Explicitly labeled R&D expense, clearly the correct mapping for research and development costs.

variables.notes: External R&D expenses is an explicit R&D line and is mapped directly to XRD.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities
candidates.why: Explicit interest expense component related to lease liabilities; clearly part of interest expense.
Interest income is the opposite sign of interest expense; may be useful as a fallback for net interest if no expense is available, but not preferred.

final_choice.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities

variables.notes: REVIEWED. "Finance costs" appears to be the primary line aggregating interest and related finance expenses, so it is chosen as the first-priority XINT row even though it was not in the candidate list. "Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities" is included as a more specific component and second priority. "Interest income" is added last as a fallback in case only net interest is available. Manual review recommended to confirm that "Finance costs" predominantly represents interest expense and that sign conventions are handled correctly. | Escape-hatch used (final_choice outside candidate list): [Income Statement] Finance costs

candidates.why: Component of equity but not the full equity of owners; using it alone would understate book equity.
Represents total equity, but there is also a duplicate-formatted "Total Equity" line; either would capture book equity.
Total equity of the entity; in absence of a specific "attributable to owners" line, this is the best proxy for book equity.
Balancing item, should not be used as the main equity measure when a proper total equity line exists.

candidates.row_label: Balancing Item - Total Liability and Equity
candidates.why: Balancing line, not a pure equity measure.
Represents total assets/liabilities+equity, not equity alone.

variables.notes: No separate line for equity attributable specifically to owners/parent is shown. "Total Equity" is therefore used as book equity. There is no indication of non-controlling interests elsewhere, so this should represent the full equity base.

No balance sheet line for non-controlling interests or minority interests appears in the preview. This suggests the entity has no minority interest; MIB is therefore set to an empty list (treated as 0).
